name: Pad Krapao Basil Stir Fry
intro: Thailand, but it’s really holy basil stir-fry that millions of Thais eat every day, all over the country.

ingredients.item: bird’s eye chilies, coarsely sliced (Thais typically use 10–12 chilies)
½-inch (1.25 cm) pieces and soaked in salted water
holy basil (krapao) leaves (the more you use, the more fragrant the dish. The wider “red” leaves are more fragrant than the
large red bird’s eye chili, sliced on the diagonal
unscented oil for frying, 1 tablespoon at a time
ground or cubed meat (I used cubed pork tenderloin because I like the meatiness of it, but ground meat takes on more of the delicious sauce)
fi sh sauce (or soy sauce, whichever you prefer. I will not go so far as to encourage the use of oyster sauce here).

method: In a mortar and pestle, mash the garlic and chilies into a paste.
Beware of fl ying chili seeds. Scrape out the mortar and set aside.
Drain the beans and basil leaves and set them in a bowl by the wok together with the sliced birds eye chilies.
Over medium-high heat, heat

1 tablespoon of the oil in a wok and fry the chili-garlic paste until fragrant. Add the meat and stir for a bit. Add the fi sh sauce, dark soy sauce and palm sugar. It should be quite saucy at this point. Taste and adjust the seasoning. Add more fi sh sauce or palm sugar if necessary. The taste should be salty and spicy but rounded with a bit of sugar. Add the beans and basil leaves. Give it a good stir, then turn off the heat.
In another pan, heat the remain- ing 1 tablespoon oil over medium- high heat and fry the eggs, one by one. The yolk should be runny and the edges should bubble up.
Serve pad krapao on a platter with steamed rice. Top with one fried egg.
Serve with the Nam Pla Prik on the side.
2 cloves garlic, peeled
2 shallots, peeled
½ inch (12.5 mm) galangal, peeled
3 fresh kaffi r lime leaves, stems removed and fi nely sliced
1 tablespoon shaved palm sugar or regular sugar
2 tablespoons potato starch
2 small bird’s eye chilies
1 lb (approx 450 g) ground beef, the higher fat the better
2 tablespoons fi sh sauce
2 tablespoons soy sauce
1 tablespoon peanut oil
Lime wedges and coriander leaves (cilantro), to serve
